label: Australian Space Agency established
description: The Australian Space Agency is officially founded in July 2018, ending a 22-year gap since the Australian Space Office was disbanded in 1996.

label: Civil Space Strategy released
description: The Australian Government releases its 10-year Civil Space Strategy 2019–2028, setting a target to triple the sector's contribution to $12 billion and create 20,000 jobs by 2030.

label: Space Agency HQ opens at Lot Fourteen
description: The Australian Space Agency's headquarters opens at Lot Fourteen in Adelaide, South Australia, co-located with the Space Discovery Centre.

label: Hayabusa2 samples return to Woomera
description: Australia supports JAXA's Hayabusa2 mission as asteroid samples from Ryugu are successfully returned to Woomera, South Australia.

label: Moon to Mars initiative announced
description: Australia commits to contributing a rover to NASA's Artemis Moon to Mars program, targeting a lunar surface mission by 2026.

label: First NASA launches from Australian soil since 1995
description: Three NASA suborbital sounding rockets launch from the Arnhem Space Centre in the Northern Territory — the first NASA launches from a commercial facility outside the US.

label: SpIRIT nanosatellite launches
description: The Australian-made SpIRIT nanosatellite launches on a SpaceX mission — the first Australian satellite to carry a foreign space agency's payload as its primary scientific instrument.

label: Bennell-Pegg graduates as astronaut
description: Katherine Bennell-Pegg becomes the first person to qualify as an astronaut under the Australian flag, completing training at the European Astronaut Centre in Germany.

label: Technology Safeguards Agreement enters into force
description: The Technology Safeguards Agreement between Australia and the US enters into force, enabling US entities to conduct space launch activities on Australian soil.

label: ELO2 chosen to build Roo-ver Moon rover
description: ELO2 is selected to design and build Australia's Roo-ver, which will travel to the Moon as part of NASA's Artemis program.

label: First Australian orbital rocket launch attempt
description: Gilmour Space Technologies attempts the first-ever launch of an Australian-made orbital rocket (Eris Test Flight 1) from Bowen Orbital Spaceport in North Queensland.
